lesson_title: Rhetoric, Diplomacy and High-Stakes Communication: Listening Lesson 4
learning_objective: Learners can critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses in a listening task about specialised discourse.
cefr_outcome: Learners can understand virtually everything heard or read and express themselves precisely in complex situations.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise listening for rhetoric, diplomacy and high-stakes communication. You will learn useful language connected to specialised discourse and practise stylistic inversion. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C2 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: stylistic inversion. Vocabulary theme: specialised discourse. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C2, learners should critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use stylistic inversion to talk about specialised discourse.
Example 2: The learner gives a C2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for C2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using stylistic inversion.
options: Correct use of stylistic inversion in a C2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with stylistic inversion.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses stylistic inversion accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of stylistic inversion.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the specialised discourse phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand specialised discourse.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand specialised discourse.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form stylistic inversion. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about specialised discourse using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: C2 Listening Check - Rhetoric, Diplomacy and High-Stakes Communication
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short listening task using stylistic inversion and vocabulary from specialised discourse.
success_criteria: Uses stylistic inversion with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to specialised discourse
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses stylistic inversion and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to specialised discourse.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about specialised discourse. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short C2 response about specialised discourse. Include the target language: stylistic inversion.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for C2-M04-L04. Speaker one: Today we are practising specialised discourse. Speaker two: I need to use stylistic inversion correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Stylistic Inversion form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for stylistic inversion
